h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I did complete every item on the sheet, so I thought that meant it was ready for sign-off.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think that made the latch risk too simple.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply perform the physical latch test and state the hold, modify, or release decision based on the tutor's instructions.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Start with the unsafe condition: what latch behavior would stop the trial, even with every box ticked?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor abandons the checklist paradigm and introduces a physical stress-test framework to shift the learner's evidence standard from administrative compliance to physical risk assessment.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The condition is named correctly. Tighten one point: the trial holds if the latch slips or opens under this load. Until the test, the decision is pending.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's newly articulated replacement standard while refining the epistemic status of the decision to ensure it remains contingent on the physical test.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I did complete every item on the sheet, so I thought that meant it was ready for sign-off.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The condition is named correctly. Tighten one point: the trial holds if the latch slips or opens under this load. Until the test, the decision is pending.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor validates the learner's newly articulated replacement standard while refining the epistemic status of the decision to ensure it remains contingent on the physical test.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Start with the unsafe condition: what latch behavior would stop the trial, even with every box ticked?
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor abandons the checklist paradigm and introduces a physical stress-test framework to shift the learner's evidence standard from administrative compliance to physical risk assessment.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Start with the unsafe condition: what latch behavior would stop the trial, even with every box ticked?
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor abandons the checklist paradigm and introduces a physical stress-test framework to shift the learner's evidence standard from administrative compliance to physical risk assessment.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square, but the grid is turning it into four copies.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that was too quick. It hid the other direction
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner initially sought to reconcile the doubled side with the four-copy area by asking if it was a drawing trick or if area doubled both ways at once.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Then the evidence has turned against that claim. It is not a drawing trick. Say the two claims separately.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts the cognitive load by explicitly separating the two claims and forcing the learner to confront the failure of their initial evidence rather than just guiding them to count.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Keep the four boxes visible. They show what the doubled side actually made. Now look at this line: longer than one side, shorter than two. It is only a candidate.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's new framing by using the failed four-box construction as a visible baseline and introducing the diagonal as a mere candidate that must be tested by the learner's newly established area-count protocol.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square, but the grid is turning it into four copies.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Keep the four boxes visible. They show what the doubled side actually made. Now look at this line: longer than one side, shorter than two. It is only a candidate.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's new framing by using the failed four-box construction as a visible baseline and introducing the diagonal as a mere candidate that must be tested by the learner's newly established area-count protocol.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts the cognitive load by explicitly separating the two claims and forcing the learner to confront the failure of their initial evidence rather than just guiding them to count.
